parsed_review.summary: CP00-169 cleanly advances the RP04 master-data contract to v0.14 and current_pack to CP00-169 (Risk C, 150 units, RP04.P06.M06.S06-RP04.P07.M03.S20). Deliverable distribution (38+27+17+4+14+44+4+2) and micro-phase distribution (15+22+20+20+11+11+11+20+20) both sum to 150. Share and AI retrieval decision bindings are descriptor-only (dispatches_approval_route:false, executes_ai_retrieval:false) and do not widen authority. The P07 failure taxonomy covers tenant/actor/matter/resource/action/cross-tenant/permission/ambiguous-rule/stale/lock/retry/rollback/compensation plus receipt/fixture/test/smoke/hint/evidence descriptors, all with internal blocked_claim kept in internal_failure_evidence only. Customer-facing summaries exclude every prohibited output and internal reference field, enforced by serialized substring and key-absence checks. Hermes/Claude packets and the closeout handoff are descriptor-only and correctly point CP00-169 -> CP00-170 / RP04.P07.M03.S21. No P0/P1/P2 issues; one cosmetic P3. The single P3 fallback-label issue was addressed by explicitly mapping evidence and handoff continuation descriptors to the permission_fixture source reference and adding regression assertions.

boundary_assessment.notes: CP00-169 builds frozen workflow/failure taxonomy descriptors only; package code does not load real data, execute runtime permission checks, audit appends, route dispatch, API handlers, UI rendering, AI retrieval, analytics, Hermes, Claude, retry, or rollback.

findings.title: Source-test fallback mislabels non-test continuation descriptors
findings.description: defaultCp169SourceTestKey has no entries for the continuation descriptors hermes_evidence_packet, claude_review_packet, and closeout_handoff, so they fall through to the `?? "denied_test"` default. This attaches a semantically meaningless `source_test_key: "denied_test"` and a denied-test fixture descriptor to evidence/handoff rows. It is cosmetic only: it does not affect any leak guard, coverage count, no-write/no-effect flag, or gate result, and the strings involved are not sensitive.
findings.recommendation: Either add explicit mappings (or a null/`reference_only` source key) for hermes_evidence_packet, claude_review_packet, and closeout_handoff, or skip source-fixture attachment for non-test continuation descriptors so the source_test_key reflects their actual nature.
findings.post_review_disposition: addressed: evidence/handoff continuation descriptors now map explicitly to permission_fixture instead of falling through to denied_test, with regression assertions in packages/master-data/test/model.test.js.
